speaker: HOTSPUR
text:
But
                ,
                 
                for
                 
                mine
                 
                own
                 
                part
                ,
                 
                my
                 
                lord
                ,
                 
                I
                 
                could
                 
                be
                
                well
                 
                contented
                 
                to
                 
                be
                 
                there
                ,
                 
                in
                 
                respect
                 
                of
                 
                the
                 
                love
                 
                I
                
                bear
                 
                your
                 
                house
                .
               He could be contented; why is he

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: not, then? In respect of the love he bears our

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: house—he shows in this he loves his own barn

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: better than he loves our house. Let me see some

speaker: HOTSPUR
text:
more. 
                The
                 
                purpose
                 
                you
                 
                undertake
                 
                is
                 
                dangerous
                .

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, that’s certain. ’Tis dangerous to take a cold,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: to sleep, to drink; but I tell you, my Lord Fool, out

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: of this nettle, danger, we pluck this flower, safety.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text:
The
                 
                purpose
                 
                you
                 
                undertake
                 
                is
                 
                dangerous
                ,
                 
                the
                 
                friends
                
                you
                 
                have
                 
                named
                 
                uncertain
                ,
                 
                the
                 
                time
                 
                itself
                 
                unsorted
                ,
                
                and
                 
                your
                 
                whole
                 
                plot
                 
                too
                 
                light
                 
                for
                 
                the
                 
                counterpoise
                
                of
                 
                so
                 
                great
                 
                an
                 
                opposition
                .
               Say you so, say you so?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I say unto you again, you are a shallow, cowardly

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: hind, and you lie. What a lack-brain is this! By

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: the Lord, our plot is a good plot as ever was laid,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: our friends true and constant—a good plot,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: good friends, and full of expectation; an excellent

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: plot, very good friends. What a frosty-spirited

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: rogue is this! Why, my Lord of York commends

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: the plot and the general course of the action.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Zounds, an I were now by this rascal, I could brain

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: him with his lady’s fan. Is there not my father, my

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: uncle, and myself, Lord Edmund Mortimer, my

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Lord of York, and Owen Glendower? Is there not

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: besides the Douglas? Have I not all their letters to

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: meet me in arms by the ninth of the next month,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: and are they not some of them set forward already?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What a pagan rascal is this—an infidel! Ha, you

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: shall see now, in very sincerity of fear and cold

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: heart, will he to the King and lay open all our

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: proceedings. O, I could divide myself and go to

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: buffets for moving such a dish of skim milk with so

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: honorable an action! Hang him, let him tell the

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: King. We are prepared. I will set forward tonight.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: How now, Kate? I must leave you within these two

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: hours.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: O my good lord, why are you thus alone?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: For what offense have I this fortnight been

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: A banished woman from my Harry’s bed?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Tell me, sweet lord, what is ’t that takes from thee

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Thy stomach, pleasure, and thy golden sleep?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Why dost thou bend thine eyes upon the earth

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And start so often when thou sit’st alone?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Why hast thou lost the fresh blood in thy cheeks

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And given my treasures and my rights of thee

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: To thick-eyed musing and curst melancholy?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: In thy faint slumbers I by thee have watched,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And heard thee murmur tales of iron wars,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Speak terms of manage to thy bounding steed,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Cry “Courage! To the field!” And thou hast talked

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Of sallies and retires, of trenches, tents,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Of palisadoes, frontiers, parapets,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Of basilisks, of cannon, culverin,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Of prisoners’ ransom, and of soldiers slain,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And all the currents of a heady fight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Thy spirit within thee hath been so at war,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And thus hath so bestirred thee in thy sleep,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: That beads of sweat have stood upon thy brow

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Like bubbles in a late-disturbèd stream,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And in thy face strange motions have appeared,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Such as we see when men restrain their breath

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: On some great sudden hest. O, what portents are these?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Some heavy business hath my lord in hand,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: And I must know it, else he loves me not.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What, ho!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Is Gilliams with the packet gone?

speaker: SERVANT
text: He is, my lord, an hour ago.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Hath Butler brought those horses from the sheriff?

speaker: SERVANT
text: One horse, my lord, he brought even now.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What horse? A roan, a crop-ear, is it not?

speaker: SERVANT
text: It is, my lord.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That roan shall be my throne.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Well, I will back him straight. O, Esperance!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Bid Butler lead him forth into the park.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: But hear you, my lord.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What say’st thou, my lady?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: What is it carries you away?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, my horse, my love, my horse.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Out, you mad-headed ape!

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: A weasel hath not such a deal of spleen

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: As you are tossed with. In faith,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: I’ll know your business, Harry, that I will.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: I fear my brother Mortimer doth stir

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: About his title, and hath sent for you

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: To line his enterprise; but if you go—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: So far afoot, I shall be weary, love.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Come, come, you paraquito, answer me

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Directly unto this question that I ask.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: In faith, I’ll break thy little finger, Harry,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: An if thou wilt not tell me all things true.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Away!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Away, you trifler. Love, I love thee not.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I care not for thee, Kate. This is no world

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To play with mammets and to tilt with lips.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: We must have bloody noses and cracked crowns,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And pass them current too.—Gods me, my horse!—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What say’st thou, Kate? What wouldst thou have with me?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Do you not love me? Do you not indeed?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Well, do not then, for since you love me not,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: I will not love myself. Do you not love me?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Nay, tell me if you speak in jest or no.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, wilt thou see me ride?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And when I am a-horseback I will swear

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I love thee infinitely. But hark you, Kate,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I must not have you henceforth question me

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Whither I go, nor reason whereabout.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Whither I must, I must; and to conclude

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This evening must I leave you, gentle Kate.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I know you wise, but yet no farther wise

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than Harry Percy’s wife; constant you are,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But yet a woman; and for secrecy

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No lady closer, for I well believe

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Thou wilt not utter what thou dost not know,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And so far will I trust thee, gentle Kate.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: How? So far?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Not an inch further. But hark you, Kate,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Whither I go, thither shall you go too.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Today will I set forth, tomorrow you.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Will this content you, Kate?

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: It must, of force.
